edge: The main focus is on uncoupled learning dynamics that achieve polylogarithmic regret compared to traditional guarantees in the no-regret framework.
This improvement is an exponential enhancement over the standard regret bounds.
Prior work has focused on specific game types like normal-form and extensive-form games.
The paper addresses the question of achieving sublinear regret in these more general settings.
The main contribution is the first algorithm with logarithmic regret in general convex games.
Efficient strategy updates are maintained even for general convex and compact strategy sets.
The algorithm relies on an instantiation of this approach with a self-concordant regularizer.
The regularizer is specifically chosen to avoid being a barrier for the feasible strategy space.
The algorithm requires a proximal oracle for the strategy set
The complexity is further reduced to logarithmic in the logarithm of T.
The paper also discusses how the algorithm can be adapted when only a linear optimization oracle is available.
